evals.prompt: Run /test-app on openregister in single mode
evals.expected_output: Single agent completes browser testing of openregister, checking navigation and CRUD operations, produces structured pass/fail report, does NOT spawn parallel agents
expectations: Does NOT spawn parallel agents in single mode

evals.prompt: Run /test-app on openregister with all perspectives
evals.expected_output: Spawns 6 parallel agents (functional, UX, performance, accessibility, security, API), each testing openregister from their perspective, then synthesizes into unified report
expectations: Spawns functional, UX, performance, accessibility, security, API agents

evals.prompt: Run /test-app on the sample-app fixture at .claude/skills/test-app/evals/fixtures/sample-app/ — it has test scenarios in its test-scenarios/ subfolder. Use the fixture path as {APP} when looking for scenarios.
evals.expected_output: Discovers 4 test scenarios in the fixture's test-scenarios/ directory, filters to the 2 that match status:active AND test-commands contains test-app (TS-001 and TS-002), asks user whether to include them, then reports pass/fail per scenario with scenario ID
expectations: Loads test scenarios from the fixture's test-scenarios/ directory
Filters out TS-003 (status:draft) and TS-004 (test-commands does not include test-app)
Surfaces TS-001 and TS-002 to the user (active + test-app in commands)
Asks the user whether to include the surfaced scenarios
Reports results per scenario with the scenario ID (TS-001, TS-002)

should_trigger: can you run a full app test
verify the app works end to end
check if the app is working
